caption:
⭐️ The Educate Together ethos explained! ⭐️

Educate Together schools are similar to other schools in Ireland; we teach the same curriculum, staff are employed/paid by the Department of Education, we have the same systems in place as other primary schools around the country etc.

What makes Educate Together schools different is the ethos and patron. Our schools are designed to be as inclusive as possible for all, regardless of ethnicity, gender, religion, ability, sexuality, socioeconomic background etc. and our ethos and daily practices reflect this. We also do not teach Religious Education, meaning we don’t have Faith Formation at school and we don’t do Sacraments in 2nd and 6th Class. Instead, we teach Learn Together (an Ethical Education curriculum) which focuses on areas such as diversity in the community/world, global citizenship, the environment, morals, world religions, spirituality and human rights.

Swipe to read more about Educate Together or comment/DM me if you have any questions and would like to learn more. 😊

If you found this post helpful, make sure to like and save so you can find it again if you decide to apply to an Educate Together school in the future! 💕

This might just be my favourite book purchase yet for our school library!! 🤩 I honestly want to do EVERY one of the lessons inside. This book is split into 6 units: Identity, Culture, Community, Empathy, Justice and Activism. Each unit then includes an introduction to that concept, a section for the kids about the meaning and importance of the specific concept, and multiple art projects (with list of materials, suggested steps and photo examples) to explore these ideas in a meaningful way with your students through art. It’s absolutely perfect for linking subjects like SPHE and Learn Together with visual art, and creates amazing opportunities for really interesting conversations in the classroom! Can’t wait to share it with the teachers in my school this year. 

#teacherfind #amazonforteachers #schoollibrary #teachershare #teachertalk #elementaryteacher #learntogether #inclusiveeducation #inclusiveclassroom #diversityandinclusion #integratedlearning #teachergram #teachersofinsta #teachersofthegram #iteachtoo

This year, I decided to book into summer courses based on some specific areas; leadership (I’ve been AP2 since 2021 and acted up as AP1 last year and DP this year), fine motor skills (particularly for middle/older classes so I can make practice a bit different, creative, fun, engaging and ‘grown up’) and gross motor skills (as teaching PE and specific gross motor/fundamental movement skills wouldn’t be my strongest area as a teacher). I’m looking forward to completing

💻 Leadership in a Digital World by @_fromtinyacorns_ 

🧵Fabric and Fibre: Sustainable Skills in the Classroom by @sew_sustainable_ 

and 

🏃‍♀️ Innovative Approaches to Teaching PE by @summercpd.ie 

#teachercpd #irishprimaryteacher #teachingandlearning #lifelonglearners #teachersforteachers

In June 2025, I was asked to act up as Deputy Principal for the year. In June 2026, I took some time to reflect upon the year and what I’ve learned along the way. 

#schoolleadership #irishprimaryteacher #teacherssupportteachers #teachertalk #teachersforteachers
